data.title: Veidekke: Contract with Bane NOR Eiendom of close to NOK 1 billion
data.description:
Bane NOR Eiendom and Veidekke have signed a contract to build theenvironmentally certified StasjonsKvartalet project that includes 234apartmentsat the new Trondheim Station. The commission is a collaborative design andbuildcontract valued at NOK 980 million excl. VAT.

"We appreciate the commission and are looking forward to extending the goodpartnership with Bane Nor Eiendom after an excellent interaction phase. We areon track with the new terminal building and a large office building atTrondheimS, both of which will be completed before the end of the year. This makes it agood fit for us to proceed with the next construction stage," says RegionalDirector Torgeir Wiig with Veidekke Bygg Trøndelag.

The station quarter that Veidekke now will build extends across two blockswitha total of 38,000 m2 of space at the train station in Trondheim. Thecommissionincludes the construction of 5,500 m2 of retail and office space towardsGryta,a new regional covered bus terminal with direct access to the central stationand a public bicycle hotel for 500 bicycles for travellers. On the basementfloor there will be parking for 140 cars. From the third level there will betworesidential quarters with 234 apartments in several buildings from three tosixstoreys in height. There are high ambitions in terms of sustainability, andtheproject will be certified in accordance with the BREEAM-NOR Very Goodenvironmental standard.

"Trondheim S is one of Norway's most ambitious hub projects, creating a modernand vibrant district with seamless mobility. With new homes, workplaces andbetter public transport solutions, we are strengthening the city'saccessibilityand contributing to sustainable urban development. We are pleased to haveVeidekke on board in this extensive project," says Project Director DagHaugdalwith Bane NOR Eiendom.

Construction works start after the summer holiday with planned completion intwostages at the start and towards the end of 2028 respectively. This contract isincluded in Veidekke's order book for the first quarter.

See more about the project here (https://stasjonskvartalet.no/).

For further information, please contact:

Regional Director Torgeir Wiig, tel. +47 95 02 07 30, torgeir.wiig@veidekke.noCommunications Manager Helge Dieset, tel. +47 90 55 33 22,helge.dieset@veidekke.no

Project Director Dag Haugdal with Bane NOR Eiendom, tel. +47 90 50 51 75,dag.haugdal@banenor.noCommunications Manager Stine Strachan with Bane NOR Eiendom, tel. +47 90 78 8968, stine.strachan@banenor.no

Veidekke's press photos (https://veidekke.fotoware.cloud/fotoweb/archives/5028-Pressebilder-Veidekke/)Subscribe to notices fromVeidekke (http://veidekke.com/no/borsmeldinger/abonner-pa-meldinger/)Veidekke (http://www.veidekke.no) is one of Scandinavia's largest contractors.In addition to undertaking all types of building and civil engineeringassignments, the group also maintains roads and produces asphalt andaggregates.Veidekke emphasises stakeholder involvement and local experience. Its annualturnover is approximately NOK 41 billion, and half of its 8,000 employees ownshares in the company. Veidekke is listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange and hasposted a profit every year since its inception in 1936.

Bane NOR Eiendom (https://www.banenoreiendom.no) is the country's leading hubdeveloper. The focus on hub development is based on a genuine environmentalcommitment and a vision of getting more people to choose the train. Bane NOREiendom has been responsible for Barcode in Bjørvika, the Grefsen Stasjonresidential project in Oslo, modern office buildings in Schweigaards gate inOslo and new commercial buildings at Drammen, Bergen, Kristiansand andTrondheimstations.

https://newsweb.oslobors.no/message/641916

data.title: Germany confiscates tanker belonging to Russian 'shadow fleet', Spiegel reports
data.description:
Germany has confiscated a decrepit tanker found adrift off its northern coast in January that is believed to be part of a shadow fleet used by Russia to circumvent oil sanctions, the Spiegel news magazine reported on Friday, citing security sources.

The Panama-flagged ship, called Eventin, was secured by German maritime authorities after being found off the Baltic Sea island of Ruegen, prompting Berlin to sharply rebuke Moscow.

Spiegel reported that a confiscation order has been issued for the tanker, meaning the vessel and its cargo of around 100,000 metric tons of oil, worth some 40 million euros ($43.33 million), now become German property.

The local customs authority and the German foreign office did not immediately respond to emailed requests for comment.

The ageing tanker, which had been travelling from Russia to Egypt, also sparked environmental concerns over a potential oil spill.

($1 = 0.9231 euros)

data.title: ASOS PLC Sees 1H Adjusted Ebitda Ahead of Consensus
ASOS PLC 1H Adjusted Ebitda Consensus Is GBP34M
Clime Investment Management Buys Back Over 19,000 Shares
data.description:
Clime Investment Management repurchased 19,424 ordinary fully-paid shares for AU$6,557 as part of an on-market buyback, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Sanlam Private Wealth acted as a broker for the transaction.

The firm proposed to buy back a maximum of 7.2 million shares under the program.

data.title: Indian rupee set for best week in 2 years on dollar inflows
data.description:
The Indian rupee inched up on Friday, maintaining its weekly upward trend, with dollar inflows helping mitigate the impact of the decline in Asian peers.

The rupee was up 0.25% at 86.1475 to the U.S. dollar at 12:16 p.m. IST, extending its weekly advance to 0.72%.

The local currency is on course for its best weekly performance in two years on the back of inflows via foreign banks and amid traders unwinding speculative long-dollar positions.

Large foreign banks were "privy" to a major flow this week and have been on the offer on dollar/rupee "all through" this week, a currency trader at a bank said.

It was likely that the inflow is related to the possible dollar funds raised in the lead to the Reserve Bank of India's next week FX swap, he said, adding that the rupee will face "stiff resistance" at the current level and "may not able to take it out".

On Friday, bankers mentioned equity inflow related to the rebalancing of the FTSE's All-World Index is likely to have boosted the rupee in the face of weak Asian cues. The rebalancing, which will take effect from Friday, is expected to garner inflows of about $1.5 billion.

The majority of Asian currencies declined on Friday, while the dollar index inched up tentatively. The primary focus for Asian currency traders remains on U.S. trade policy and the trajectory of U.S. economic growth.

data.title: Pengana International Equities Buys Back Shares
data.description:
Pengana International Equities repurchased 130,214 shares for AU$152,748 in an on-market buyback, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The investment management company proposes to buy back a total of 257.1 million shares, per the filing.

Bell Potter was the broker for the buyback.

data.title: ASOS PLC 1H Adjusted Ebitda Margin Consensus Is 2.6%
ANA Previously Projected That Acquisition Would Be Completed on March 31
Turkey Consumer Confidence at Near 2-Year High
data.description:
The consumer confidence index in Turkey climbed to 85.9 in March 2025 from 82.1 in the previous month.

This marked the highest reading since May 2023, as expectations continued to improve regarding households' financial situation for the next twelve months (84.7 vs 83.1 in February) and expectations for the general economic situation for the same period (84.6 vs 79.3).

However, spending propensity for the year ahead (100.2 vs 100.3) edged lower.

Meanwhile, consumers were less pessimistic about their current financial situations (70.9 vs 65.6).

data.title: Munich Re's Bolt-On Deal via Its Ergo Division Should Be Appreciated — Market Talk
data.description: Munich Re subsidiary Ergo's acquisition of a business it has been involved in for a while is reassuring, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods says after the primary insurance arm announced the acquisition of the shares it doesn't already own in U.S. digital small-business insurer Next. The deal values the business at $2.6 billion. Ergo has been involved in Next a year after the group was founded in 2016 and had a 29% stake. "We think investors should appreciate that the group is beginning to convert its excess capital (currently earning almost nothing on the balance sheet) into an earnings stream, particularly as the deal economics appear reasonable at first glance," analyst Darius Satkauskas writes. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Heathrow Airport Closes for Day Because of Power Outage — WSJ
data.description:
By Hannah Miao

London's Heathrow Airport, an international hub and major gateway for American travelers to Europe, was shut down for the day and expects significant disruption in the days to come because of a power outage.

Heathrow will be closed until 11:59 p.m. local time on Friday, according to a statement on the airport's website. Heathrow advised passengers not to travel to the airport and to contact their airlines for more information.

The outage was caused by a fire at an electrical substation supplying the airport, according to Heathrow.

Heathrow doesn't know when power will be reliably restored and will provide an update when more information is available, a spokesperson said.

"We expect significant disruption over the coming days and passengers should not travel to the airport under any circumstances until the airport reopens," the spokesperson said.

Friday's closure affects at least 1,300 flights scheduled to and from Heathrow, according to flight tracker Flightradar24.

As of early Friday morning local time, 120 aircraft already in the air would need to divert to alternate airports or return to their origins, according to Flightradar24.

The fire broke out late Thursday evening at an electrical substation in western London, according to the London Fire Brigade. Around 70 firefighters were battling the fire, which also caused a power outage affecting many homes and local businesses, the brigade said.

The cause of the fire is currently unknown, according to the brigade.

Heathrow was the fourth-busiest airport in the world last year ranked by capacity with more than 51.5 million seats, according to data provider OAG. Last year, 83.9 million passengers traveled through Heathrow, according to the airport.

Write to Hannah Miao at hannah.miao@wsj.com

data.title: REG-Form 8.5 (EPT/RI)-Dowlais Group plc
data.description:
FORM 8.5 (EPT/RI)

PUBLIC DEALING DISCLOSURE BY AN EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER WITH RECOGNISED INTERMEDIARY STATUS DEALING IN A CLIENT-SERVING CAPACITY

Rule 8.5 of the Takeover Code (the “Code”)

1.        KEY INFORMATION

(a)        Name of exempt principal trader:Investec Bank plc
(b)        Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:         Use a separate form for each offeror/offereeDowlais Group Plc        
(c)        Name of the party to the offer with which exempt principal trader is connected:Investec is Broker to Dowlais Group Plc
(d)        Date dealing undertaken:20th March 2025
(e)        In addition to the company in 1(b) above, is the exempt principal trader making disclosures in respect of any other party to this offer?         If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state “N/A”N/A

2.        DEALINGS BY THE EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER

Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 2(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.

The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.

(a)        Purchases and sales

(c)        Stock-settled derivative transactions (including options)

3.        OTHER INFORMATION

(a)        Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer: Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included. If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state “none”
  • None

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to: (i)        the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or (ii)        the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced: If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state “none”
  • None
Date of disclosure:21st March 2025
Contact name:Priyali Bhattacharjee
Telephone number:+91 9768034903

Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service.

The Panel’s Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code’s dealing disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.

The Code can be viewed on the Panel’s website at www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk.

data.title: Briscoe Group's Performance Rights Lapse
data.description:
Briscoe Group said that around 111,358 performance rights issued on August 5, 2022, lapsed, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Following this lapse, the company has 483,017 Performance Rights remaining on issue.

data.title: MTQ Repurchases More Shares
data.description:
MTQ repurchased 20,000 shares for SG$5,230 or SG$0.26 per share on Friday, according to a same-day filing on the Singapore Exchange.

So far, the engineering and oilfield services firm has repurchased around 1.5 million shares under its current share buyback mandate.

data.title: Murakami-Linked City Index Eleventh And Related Shareholders Raise Stake In Ferrotech Holdings To 8.17% From 6.40%, Filing Shows
data.description:
Ferrotec Holdings Corp :

data.title: South Korean Shares Edge Higher Amid Possible Position Adjustments — Market Talk
data.description: South Korea's Kospi edged 0.2% higher to close at 2643.13, erasing earlier losses amid possible position adjustments. Investors seem to have reconsidered economic risks and the Fed's stance on inflation and growth, Saxo Markets' APAC Research team says in commentary. Also, U.S. President Trump has intensified pressure on the Fed to lower rates, which boosts expectations of market volatility, the team adds. Among the best performers on the benchmark index are Doosan Enerbility, which climbed 5.9%, Posco International, which added 5.7%, and Doosan Bobcat, which was 3.4% higher. Meanwhile, Hanwha Aerospace tumbled 13.0% after announcing a plan to raise KRW3.6 trillion through a share sale. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)
